extraction.one_line_summary: KWIATKOWSKI won Stage 18 after CARAPAZ took wrong side of roundabout to attack mid-Col de la Madeleine, then Kwiatkowski bridged late on descent to outsprint Carapaz.
tactical_patterns: CARAPAZ used roundabout geometry mid-climb to create gap, demonstrating non-traditional attack points work on technical alpine roads.
KWIATKOWSKI bridged to leader late on descent rather than climbing, showing teammates can convert leader attacks into own wins.
CARAPAZ took polka-dot jersey via intermediate sprints with HERSHEY, revealing KOM competition tactics during breakaway influenced stage outcome.
ROGLIC dropped KUSS on final climb despite being teammates, signaling GC dominance messaging before time trial.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: First Grand Tour stage win at age 30 after 10 Grand Tours suggests late-career resurgence, bridged descent not climb
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Won KOM jersey via aggressive intermediate sprint tactics with teammate support, nearly won stage with roundabout attack
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Dropped out of top-5 GC on hardest alpine stage, exposed limits on highest vertical gain stages
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Moved to 5th GC on maximum vertical stage, signal of specific strength on accumulative climbing days
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Rode final climb alone ahead of domestique KUSS, dominance display before time trial rather than conserving energy
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Beat SAGAN cleanly in intermediate sprint, confirmed as fastest pure sprinter despite mountain stage context
